hero_subtitle: Over two decades of walking alongside the most vulnerable children and families in Kisumu, West Kenya.
story_title: Born from a Vision for Children
story_lead: The Uhuru Community Development Project (UCDP) was founded in 2002 by Davies Okombo, a professional and trained Kenyan social worker, with a clear and urgent mission: to support destitute, hopeless, homeless, and defenseless children in Kisumu, West Kenya.
story_p1: The name "Uhuru" — meaning freedom in Swahili — reflects the core belief that every child deserves to live and grow in freedom and dignity, regardless of their circumstances.
story_p2: UCDP is a politically and denominationally independent non-profit organisation, officially registered with Kenya's Ministry of Gender, Sports, Culture and Social Services as a Community Based Organisation (CBO). Our board consists of 5 permanent members, and practical implementation is led by our founder, Davies Okombo.

timeline.title: UCDP Founded
timeline.text: Davies Okombo establishes the organisation in Manyatta, Kisumu, to support street children and vulnerable families.

timeline.title: Internship Programme Launched
timeline.text: UCDP begins hosting student interns from Germany, building international partnerships and enriching programme delivery.

timeline.title: Partnership with Uhuru e.V. (Germany)
timeline.text: The German support association "Uhuru e.V. – Gemeinsam für Kinder in Kenia" is founded in Tübingen, providing crucial international backing.

timeline.title: Expansion of Programmes
timeline.text: Launch of the Kindergarten, Safe House, Community Clinic, and Skills Training programmes, reaching thousands more beneficiaries.

timeline.title: 20+ Years of Impact
timeline.text: UCDP continues to serve the Kisumu community through 8 active projects, touching thousands of lives across Manyatta and beyond.

values.title: Community-First
values.text: We work alongside communities, not above them. Every decision is grounded in the real needs of the people we serve.

values.title: Child Protection
values.text: The safety, dignity, and wellbeing of every child is non-negotiable — it sits at the heart of everything we do.

values.title: Sustainability
values.text: We focus on long-term, lasting change through education, skills, and community empowerment.

values.title: Empowerment
values.text: We believe in equipping individuals with tools and knowledge to shape their own futures with confidence.

values.title: Partnership
values.text: We collaborate openly with local volunteers, international partners, and community members to multiply impact.

values.title: Independence
values.text: We are politically and denominationally independent — serving all people in our community without discrimination.
